exclusion_rules.description: Mission Indradhanush is a catch-up immunization drive that primarily targets children under 2 years; later intensified rounds (IMI) extended catch-up vaccination to children up to about 5-6 years. Pregnant women who missed Tetanus-Diphtheria (Td) doses are also covered regardless of age, so this exclusion never applies to a pregnant woman. Older children are covered through the routine Universal Immunization Programme rather than this drive.

family_criteria.family_definition: Universal catch-up drive — every child who is unvaccinated or partially vaccinated (primarily under 2 years, extended to about 5-6 years in intensified rounds) and every pregnant woman who has missed vaccine doses is eligible, regardless of income, caste, or BPL status. Focus is on those left out or dropped out of routine immunization, including hard-to-reach, migrant, and underserved areas.

covered_procedures: Free catch-up vaccination with all vaccines under the Universal Immunization Programme for children who missed or dropped out of routine doses
BCG, OPV/IPV (polio), Hepatitis B, Pentavalent (DPT + Hep B + Hib), Rotavirus, PCV, Measles-Rubella (MR), and JE (in endemic districts) as due
DPT and Td booster doses for eligible children
Tetanus-Diphtheria (Td) vaccination for unvaccinated or partially vaccinated pregnant women
Special focus on reaching unvaccinated and partially vaccinated children in low-coverage, hard-to-reach, migrant, urban slum, and tribal areas
Intensified rounds of immunization sessions conducted over multiple consecutive days
Free of cost, delivered through additional special immunization sessions alongside routine UIP
excluded_procedures: Vaccines outside the National Immunization Schedule / UIP basket
Treatment of diseases (the drive is preventive vaccination only)
Fully immunized children who have no due or missed doses

required_documents.name: Mother and Child Protection (MCP) Card
required_documents.notes: Helps identify which doses are missed; not mandatory. ASHA workers also identify left-out and dropped-out children through head-counts and due-lists.

required_documents.name: Aadhaar / Birth proof
alternatives: No ID needed for the vaccine
required_documents.notes: For digital registration on U-WIN; absence of ID cannot be a reason to deny catch-up vaccination.

enrollment_channels: Special Mission Indradhanush immunization sessions in villages and urban wards
Mobile and outreach vaccination teams in hard-to-reach areas
ASHA / ANM identification of left-out and dropped-out children
enrollment_steps: Step 1: ASHA/ANM workers conduct head-counts and prepare due-lists of unvaccinated and partially vaccinated children and pregnant women
Step 2: Special intensified immunization sessions are scheduled (multiple rounds, each over several consecutive days)
Step 3: Families are mobilised and informed of the special session dates
Step 4: Bring the child or pregnant woman to the Mission Indradhanush session at the nearest site
Step 5: All due/missed vaccines are administered free of cost per the National Immunization Schedule
Step 6: Doses recorded in the MCP card and on the U-WIN registry
Step 7: Families are reminded of subsequent rounds to complete full immunization
description_for_embedding: Mission Indradhanush (MI), launched by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare in December 2014, is a flagship catch-up immunization drive under the Universal Immunization Programme that aims to fully immunize all children and pregnant women who were left out or dropped out of routine vaccination. It primarily targets children under 2 years and pregnant women, with Intensified Mission Indradhanush (IMI, launched October 2017) and its later rounds (IMI 5.0) extending catch-up vaccination to children up to about five years and focusing on districts and urban areas with low immunization coverage. The drive provides, completely free of cost, all vaccines in the National Immunization Schedule — BCG, polio (OPV/IPV), Hepatitis B, Pentavalent, Rotavirus, Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine, Measles-Rubella, Japanese Encephalitis (in endemic districts), and DPT/Td boosters — plus Tetanus-Diphtheria vaccination for unvaccinated or partially vaccinated pregnant women. Special intensified immunization sessions are run over multiple consecutive days, with extra focus on hard-to-reach, migrant, urban-slum, and tribal populations. ASHA and ANM workers identify left-out and dropped-out children through head-counts and due-lists. There is no income, caste, or BPL restriction and no fee. The mission has vaccinated crores of children and pregnant women and was showcased internationally as a best practice for rapidly raising full-immunization coverage.
